method_atom: A general decomposition scheme that, given a space with an arbitrary notion of parallel transport (a connection that need not preserve any metric structure or be symmetric), and given a projection operator onto a subspace that need not be orthogonal or of any fixed codimension, constructs the natural derived quantities of that split: two distinct second-fundamental-form-type tensors that coincide when the projection is orthogonal, plus a family of mixed cross-projected curvature quantities, and expresses the full curvature and its integrability (cyclic/differential) identities as sums of pieces tangential and transverse to the subspace.
object_atom: The affine and Riemannian geometry of spacetime manifolds in general relativity and unified field theories, applied to perfect fluids, timelike and string congruences, degenerate (lightlike) curves and surfaces, and the 3+1 spacelike initial-value formulation.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the subspace-projection decomposition of curvature and its identities under an arbitrary connection and tilted, possibly degenerate projection; the domain-specific part is the relativistic spacetime and its matter configurations to which it is applied.
